title: Habenaria chlorantha
taxonomy: Habenaria chlorantha is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family consists of approximately 30,000 known species, with Habenaria chlorantha being a part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. The genus Habenaria, to which Habenaria chlorantha belongs, is a group of terrestrial orchids native to various parts of the world. intro: Habenaria chlorantha, commonly known as green orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). Traditionally, Habenaria chlorantha has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria chlorantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Habenaria chlorantha is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or syrup, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds.
